title: Zillow Revenue 2012-2025 Historical Trends
text: Zillow Historical Revenue and Growth Trends. Long-Term Revenue Trajectory: Zillow Group's revenue has demonstrated significant growth over the 2012-2025 period, reflecting the expansion of its business segments and the growth of the online real estate marketplace. Key Growth Periods: 2020-2021 saw exceptional revenue growth driven by pandemic-related housing market activity and low interest rates. 2022-2023 experienced a cooling period following the iBuying exit and interest rate increases. 2024-2025 showed recovery and diversification across mortgages and rentals segments. Business Evolution: The company evolved from a primarily advertising-based model (Premier Agent segment) to a more diversified model including mortgages, rentals, and emerging AI services. Segment Contribution: Internet, Media & Technology (IMT): Roughly 70% of revenue, relatively stable but maturing. Mortgages: Rapidly growing from near-zero to significant contribution (10-15% of revenue). Rentals: Fastest-growing segment, expanding from less than 10% to 20%+ of revenue. Market Factors: The revenue trends were influenced by: Interest rate changes impacting housing transaction volume and mortgage demand. Housing inventory and affordability dynamics. Competitive pressure and market share dynamics. Regulatory environment and antitrust scrutiny.
